body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/18287579/223383996/1543981737?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OTdmYzliMy1hZmM3LWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwM2JkYTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EjoAvWRuBTOdsaEOgMtuOpVVfGcU9NhsvobBnEph1Fw=&emci=487fc9b3-afc7-ea11-9b05-00155d03bda0&emdi=497fc9b3-afc7-ea11-9b05-00155d03bda0&ceid=11809611)
Coronavirus cases are surging worse than ever before in countless states across the country, including right here in Mississippi, and what does this administration do? **They attack coronavirus task force member Dr. Anthony Fauci for telling the truth about the seriousness of this pandemic and warning that we must do more to stop it.** Not only are they trying to silence and discredit this well-respected infectious disease expert, but this week we learned that _the administration has told our nation’s hospitals to stop sending COVID-19 data to the Centers for Disease Control_ and to instead send it directly to the administration themselves, potentially stopping this data from becoming public knowledge. It’s clear what’s happening here: This administration is scrambling to do whatever it takes to spin the facts and make it seem like this pandemic isn’t as bad as it really is. With workers, students, teachers, and families across the country looking for leadership and worrying about their health and safety as new cases and deaths rise, this is a dangerous and inappropriate way for our executive branch to act. **We need to listen to medical experts like Dr. Fauci and the folks at the CDC, and put the health of Americans before partisan goals, period.** [**If you agree, add your name to join me in standing with science and Dr. Fauci today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/18287580/223383997/1925774011?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OTdmYzliMy1hZmM3LWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwM2JkYTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EjoAvWRuBTOdsaEOgMtuOpVVfGcU9NhsvobBnEph1Fw=&emci=487fc9b3-afc7-ea11-9b05-00155d03bda0&emdi=497fc9b3-afc7-ea11-9b05-00155d03bda0&ceid=11809611) | | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/18287581/223383998/1925774011?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OTdmYzliMy1hZmM3LWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwM2JkYTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EjoAvWRuBTOdsaEOgMtuOpVVfGcU9NhsvobBnEph1Fw=&emci=487fc9b3-afc7-ea11-9b05-00155d03bda0&emdi=497fc9b3-afc7-ea11-9b05-00155d03bda0&ceid=11809611)

Peter,
On Tuesday, Sara Gideon won the Democrat primary to be my opponent this November. But something else also happened, something that has never occurred in the history of our democracy... she was given $4 million dollars for winning.
_**Let me explain.**_
In 2018, when Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh was up for confirmation, several Far Left groups got together to try and buy my vote -- if I voted for Justice Kavanaugh they would award my future opponent a fund that is now valued at $4 million dollars. If I voted against him, the money would be refunded and just disappear.
My vote can not and will not ever be bought -- something you know full well.
My vote that day in 2018 put a bullseye on my campaign, and has now put my race at a $4 million dollar disadvantage.
**I voted to confirm Brett Kavanaugh, and it 's a vote I stand by. I voted for fairness, due process, and the rule of law. But right now I need to know if you will stand by me.**
**_Yes, Susan! You 've got my support!_

name: =?utf-8?Q?Dave=20Lindstrom?=
subject: Enough is Enough!
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/lindstromforsenate.com/enough-is-enough?e=41f8e27552)
---
| |
---
| |
# I’ve had enough!

Enough of watching our cities being destroyed. Enough of our small businesses – and even large businesses - being looted. Enough of our country’s heritage being destroyed by angry mobs. Enough of our police being under attack. Enough of anarchists trying to destroy our capitalistic system.

That is why as your next U.S. senator, I will introduce legislation to make these acts of domestic terrorism a federal crime, punishable by a mandatory 10-year prison sentence. We need strong laws on the books to address these crimes and harsh punishments that serve as a deterrent. We cannot as a country sit idly by any longer and let this happen. Especially when so many local officials are either powerless to act or vocally supporting these acts.

We’re seeing far left local officials actually fanning the flames and pushing for these actions. A city council member in Seattle just called for replacing capitalism with socialism. New York’s mayor is focusing on replacing our national heritage while violent crime in his city skyrockets. AOC dismisses a rise in murder as people just trying to find bread. Here in Kansas, Lawrence’s mayor undermined the police chief by reversing his decision to remove people who illegally blocked off a main street with couches and things to stage a sit-in and deface public property.

This legislation is needed and needed now. As your senator I fight for this bill since we need to say enough is enough!

Dave

_P.S. – As we enter the final few weeks, I could use your help in getting my message across to all Kansans. Please click on the logo below to see how you can help._

Friends,

We have come to an important moment in the campaign: our race was just named a Red-to-Blue seat by the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee, the organization which helps flip House seats. **It is now one of 30 targeted races across the country and the only target in Florida.**

This is big news because we win by building coalitions of support and the DCCC is a must-have organization in our coalition! Together, so many of us are putting in the work to bring change to Florida's 16th district and I am grateful to everyone and every organization who has stepped up to help. **[If you are as excited about this milestone as I am, we'd so appreciate a contribution to show your support!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/18288736/223408357/1073285021?emci=c9c367ea-90c7-ea11-9b05-00155d03bda0&emdi=195694bc-b9c7-ea11-9b05-00155d03bda0&contactdata=T7yoVzuQk1sNjrosoPbeaT0U4rMF0yiiuprJ9j5j3iuU7VRI%2BlnVyirzyxT7VNPFA59qNqr1olC1LvORnDSwW8rFuLFpApZwMyVeohYLGUTt1%2BdMW54kMpyU5jiyuXkXmjn9CxsEXEIHEMim4oniCQ%3D%3D&amount=25&refcode=071620-es-ihc-red-to-blue-kicker&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODIwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxOTU2OTRiYy1iOWM3LWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwM2JkYTAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QhPICDopb9dMwhjEUWXqKX-lcBqnk5t2B4DVV0XL6t8=) **

Thank you for your support, Darin LaHood | | | Paid for by LaHood for Congress | | P.O. Box 10735 Peoria, IL 61612 | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | |

name: =?utf-8?Q?Sara=20Gideon?=
subject: On Tuesday, I accepted the Democratic nomination for United States Senate in Maine.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Gideon for Maine
body:
| | | |
---
| | | Friends -

On Tuesday, I accepted the Democratic nomination for United States Senate in Maine. That means Senator Collins is our official Republican opponent — _the race is on_. I’ll be honest, the next few months will not be easy. Susan Collins is a 24-year incumbent with the entire Republican Party establishment behind her. She’s stood with Donald Trump and Mitch McConnell when they needed her most, including as a critical vote to pass a massive corporate tax giveaway, and to confirm Brett Kavanaugh to the Supreme Court. Outside interests will spend nearly unlimited amounts of money to support her. But if we are in this together, we are going to win. And the word _together_ is key, because I cannot do this alone. So I am asking: [**Can I count on you to make a donation to our campaign now? I’m rejecting corporate PAC money, so we’re trying to generate as many individual donations as we can.**](https://SaraGideon.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e073bbccd4332535d013bb797&id=cbc18d49db&e=27a313c857 "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-july-2020?refcode=em200716-ntl") Like I said, this won’t be easy. Our race is critical if Democrats are going to take back the Senate, so Mitch McConnell and conservative special interest groups are already spending millions of dollars to save Collins’ campaign. But because of supporters like you, we are in a really strong position to defeat them in November and bring Democrats one step closer to the Senate majority — as long as we keep pushing. So please, be one of the first to make a contribution now that I am the official Democratic nominee. You can chip in here: [**Act.SaraGideon.com/TheRaceIsOn**](https://SaraGideon.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e073bbccd4332535d013bb797&id=047fbbf657&e=27a313c857 "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sg-july-2020?refcode=em200716-ntl") I am so proud of all we’ve accomplished since we launched this campaign last year. We’ve held Suppers with Sara, our campaign’s version of a town hall, in 19 cities and towns across Maine. We have organizers reaching Mainers in every single county, and I’ve met with thousands of voters in their own communities. And along the way, we proved that a campaign funded by the grassroots and NOT corporate PAC money can stay competitive in this race. We've come a long way since we launched last year — but we still have a long way to go until this race is ours. That's why I'm glad to know you'll be with me every step of the way. Thank you, Sara Gideon
---
| [CONTRIBUTE ](https://SaraGideon.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e073bbccd4332535d013bb797&id=27e3531be7&e=27a313c857 "CONTRIBUTE ")
---
|
---
| | |
---
| Paid for by Sara Gideon for Maine

Email is a very important tool for our campaign, but if you'd like to stop receiving messages from the campaign you can **[unsubscribe here.](https://SaraGideon.us3.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=e073bbccd4332535d013bb797&id=ffa74c3c2e&e=27a313c857&c=26fcafc008)**

Our campaign doesn't accept corporate PAC money, so if you can make a contribution today, it'd mean a lot. **[Chip in here](https://SaraGideon.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e073bbccd4332535d013bb797&id=338b31eba7&e=27a313c857)**.
---

name: =?utf-8?B?VGVhbSBTw6FuY2hleg==?=
subject: Hold ICE Accountable >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sánchez |
body:
In an effort to curb the spread of COVID-19, ICE staff have resulted to using harsh, disinfectant chemicals in detention facilities, causing itchy rashes, bloody noses, burning eyes, fainting, and coughing fits for people in ICE custody.

This is nothing short of a human rights violation. These reports must be taken seriously by Congress.

That's why Linda has co-signed a petition calling on Congress to open an investigation into ICE's COVID-19 response. Will you stand up for the people in ICE custody by signing your name?

Thank you for taking action,

-- Team Sánchez

----------------------------------------
| Paid for by Stand with Sánchez |
----------------------------------------

415 New Jersey Avenue, SE - Unit 1

name: MJ Hegar Senior Staff
subject: The biggest battleground state by the numbers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
Winning Texas is going to take a whole lot of work.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890114/7f51f7bc/41f15c43/4ef66c21/1272101302/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | **[HELP MJ FLIP THE BIGGEST BATTLEGROUND STATE>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890114/7f51f7bc/41f15c43/4ef66c20/1272101302/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MzEifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?refcode=email_20200716.2nd__1731__&amount=10")** Peter -- Texas is the biggest battleground state in the nation -- **but what does that really mean?**
* We have a voting-age population of 21,596,071.
* We have 16 million registered voters (which means **5 MILLION** people aren't registered to vote yet).
* Polls show 34% of registered voters aren't sure who they're supporting in the Senate election. That's an estimated **5.4 MILLION** people. **[Help us reach them>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890114/7f51f7bc/41f15c43/4ef66c20/1272101302/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MzEifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?refcode=email_20200716.2nd__1731__&amount=10")**
* **Right now, polls put MJ within single digits of beating Sen. Cornyn and winning in November.**

All of that means two things. First of all, MJ **_can absolutely_** win in November. Second, it's going to take a whole lot of work to reach all the unregistered, undecided, and swing voters we need to win Texas and flip the Senate.

**Texas is a big state, and it 's going to take a Texas-sized grassroots team to win! [Make a donation to give MJ the resources she'll need.](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890114/7f51f7bc/41f15c43/4ef66c20/1272101302/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MzEifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?refcode=email_20200716.2nd__1731__&amount=10")**

\-- Team MJ

**[DONATE](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890114/7f51f7bc/41f15c43/4ef66c20/1272101302/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MzEifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?refcode=email_20200716.2nd__1731__&amount=10")**
---

|
---

| It's clear we need a real leader for Texas and a Senate that prioritizes our health care. **[More than ever, we're relying on online fundraising to keep building the campaign we need to win in November, and we greatly appreciate your continued support>>](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890114/7f51f7bc/41f15c43/4ef66c27/1272101302/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3MzEifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-covidfooter?refcode=1731&amount=10")**

However, we know this is not an easy time for many. If you'd like to pause receiving fundraising emails for the next few weeks, **[please let us know here](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890114/7f51f7bc/41f15c43/4ef66c26/1272101302/VEsB/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/s/mj-covid-fewer-emails")**. We'll still keep in touch with important information and to ask your thoughts from time to time.

“Your favorite independent venue is at risk of closing forever, but together we can help.” - LADY GAGA

“OutKast started in indie music venues and we believe in their importance in the careers of artists.” - OUTKAST

“This is a chance for us ALL to consider the things we miss the most.” - JUSTIN VERNON of BON IVER

“Independent venues need our help. These include clubs that blink-182 and many of our friends got our start in and we still frequent.” - BLINK 182

It’s especially bad at the New York Times, the newspaper that tried to cancel Tom because Tom supports law and order and opposes violent riots, arson, and looting.

Check out Tom's Fox News editorial on the danger of cancel culture and why the woke mob shouldn’t be put in charge.

Stand With Tom http://links.mail1.digitalcampaignstrategies.com/ctt?ms=NDI5NzM1NzUS1&kn=10&r=ODQ4NzI0NzcxNjgxS0&b=2&j=MTgwMTIzNjA0MAS2&mt=1&rt=0 Bari Weiss' NY Times exit shows stifling political correctness left wants. Don't let them winThe woke mob at The New York Times has claimed another scalp. Earlier this week, opinion columnist and editor Bari Weiss resigned from the paper after being viciously harassed by her colleagues for daring on occasion to express conservative opinions in print.

This is not a one-off event but a trend, as far-left, anti-American forces have consolidated control over some of our nation’s most influential institutions.

Weiss followed in the footsteps of former opinion editor James Bennet, a liberal who was forced to resign by The Times’ publisher for running my op-ed.

I argued that the military could be called out as a last resort to restore public order in cities wracked by violent rioting and looting—an opinion shared at the time by a majority of Americans.

The Times claimed the op-ed didn’t meet its “standards.” (I agree: my op-ed far exceeded its normal sophomoric fare.) But the paper couldn’t identify a single falsehood or error that explained why the op-ed deserved almost two weeks of ritual denunciation and hyperventilation—or why Bennet deserved to lose his job.

Maybe it’s only because the publisher can’t fire himself.

The truth is that Weiss, Bennet, and others like them aren’t being harassed and forced from their jobs due to a genuine failing on their part. What they’re really guilty of is failing to conform to the searing standards of political correctness that have been imposed by far-left activists at The New York Times and other elite institutions.

Just as radicals in many cities have seized control of the streets and are demolishing statues to American heroes like George Washington, a band of more polished radicals have seized control of venerable institutions and are using their newfound power to bully an unwilling country.

Suffice it to say the New York Times isn’t a newspaper anymore, in the traditional sense of the word. It’s a propaganda bullhorn—a super PAC for the Democratic Party, the 1619 Project, and other revolutionary causes. Perhaps it should rebrand as The New Woke Times.

The media is just the latest prize in the left’s long march through elite cultural institutions. This march began on college campuses, where radical faculty—many of whom had participated in the mayhem and bloodshed of the 1960s and ‘70s—indoctrinated students with far-left ideology and sent them off to tear down supposedly oppressive institutions like the family and the police, and harangue those who disagree.

It has become commonplace for conservative students, faculty, and speakers to be shouted down and even assaulted on campus by far-left student radicals, while administrators look the other way—or worse, join in their persecution.

Those student radicals graduated and got degrees, but they didn’t grow up or go away. They entered the real world armed with protest tactics and elite credentials—and they’re using both to upend American society, from sports leagues to social-media feeds.

Their primary objective is to impose a stifling political correctness on the American public. They must not be allowed to succeed.

These radicals are now in charge of social-media companies like Twitter, which routinely censors conservatives, up to and including the president. And they’re in charge of big corporations like the NBA, which just announced a list of pre-approved protest slogans players can wear on their jerseys.

Curiously, the list didn’t include any slogans that would offend the Chinese Communist Party, which controls the league’s access to the vast Chinese market; woke radicals seem not to care nearly so much about actual oppression committed by Communists overseas. Or at least, they can be paid not to care.

Far-left absurdities about race, sex, and other topics seemed harmless enough when they were parroted by impressionable freshmen in social-justice seminars.

Now it’s clear these radicals aren’t just a group of special snowflakes on a college campus. And they aren’t content protesting outside the dean’s office and publishing in obscure journals. They’ve gotten ahold of real power and seek to use that power to remodel the country so their absurd beliefs are the only acceptable beliefs that can be voiced in public.

The elites have nearly finished purging their ranks of dissenters like Weiss and Bennet, who believe in such quaint principles as pluralism and free speech.

Now they can turn their full attention to silencing, disenfranchising, and defunding the overwhelming majority of Americans who vote or think the “wrong” way—who believe, for example, that there are only two sexes, or that abortion is wrong, or that the police make us safe, or that our country is fundamentally good and noble.

That’s why increasingly we hear calls for such radical proposals as eliminating the Senate filibuster, packing the Supreme Court, and abolishing the Electoral College.

Having consolidated control over the media, higher education, and big corporations, the left is enraged that it cannot permanently capture our political institutions. So they want to rig the rules to take care of that, too.

Americans of all races, religions, and walks of life who believe in the principles of this country—natural equality, toleration, the rule of law—must realize that these principles are under threat, and band together to protect them against the powerful elites revolting against them, and ultimately against us. Paid for by Cotton for Senate 2226 Cottondale Lane
Suite 200
Little Rock, AR 72202

Senator Cotton was honorably discharged from the U.S. Army. Images do not imply endorsement by the Department of Defense or any service branch.

name: Finance Update (via ChristyForCongress.org)
subject: re: Christy's candidate statement
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| | [](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bab8/29776869/9e6d3d3/569efd08/815280412/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTZfbW0zLXMxIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200716_mm3-s1") We've got one more day to raise the $17,000 Christy needs to file her candidate statement, Peter.

If we don't hit this goal, we'll have some tough choices to make. We might have to pull Christy away from campaign events with voters to spend time fundraising -- which means less time showing the district why she is the candidate for the people.

We simply can't afford to fall short.

**[We're still $6,073 away from our goal. Can you pitch in $5 or more now to close the gap?](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bab8/29776869/9e6d3d3/569efd08/815280412/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTZfbW0zLXMxIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200716_mm3-s1")**

[Express Donate $50](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bab8/29776869/9e6d3d3/569efd0b/815280412/VEsD/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTZfbW0zLXMxIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200716_mm3-s1&express_lane=true&amount=50") | [Express Donate $100](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bab8/29776869/9e6d3d3/569efd04/815280412/VEsA/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTZfbW0zLXMxIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200716_mm3-s1&express_lane=true&amount=100")
[Express Donate $250](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bab8/29776869/9e6d3d3/569efd05/815280412/VEsB/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTZfbW0zLXMxIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200716_mm3-s1&express_lane=true&amount=250") | [Donate Other Amount](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bab8/29776869/9e6d3d3/569efd08/815280412/VEsO/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTZfbW0zLXMxIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200716_mm3-s1")
In a race as competitive as ours, every single dollar counts. We make campaign plans down to the last cent -- and the strength of our fundraising determines everything from how many texts we can send to remind folks to vote, to how often Christy is able to attend virtual events to connect with voters at home.

Peter, raising $6,073 before midnight tomorrow might seem like a stretch. But here's the thing: When hundreds of supporters chip in what they can, when they can, whether that's $3 or $25 or $50, it starts to add up.

**[Anything you give will get this team one step closer to hitting our $17,000 filing goal. Please, chip in $5 or more now to keep our campaign on track to win in November.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6bab8/29776869/9e6d3d3/569efd08/815280412/VEsP/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MTZfbW0zLXMxIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2007_email_christy_csgoal?refcode=em20_200716_mm3-s1")**

Thanks,

Lexie

Finance Director
Christy Smith for Congress

Now she needs our help to defeat Mike Garcia and flip this top pick-up House seat blue in November. Help elect a champion for our health care, our schools, and our rights. name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: This is how much overregulation costs your family each year
body:
For years, the Swamp has interfered in the lives of private American citizens by piling regulations on nearly every form of economic activity. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=32fc801719&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • July 16, 2020
---
| | |
# **This is how much overregulation costs your family each year**

**For years, the** **Swamp** has interfered in the lives of private American citizens by piling regulations on nearly every form of economic activity.

**This “expert” rule** from Washington has created thousands of well-paying jobs for bureaucrats while costing everyone else time, money, and—for many—their careers.

**This afternoon,** President Trump is announcing more results from his Administration’s historic regulatory relief efforts _._ The White House Council of Economic Advisers (CEA), for example, estimates that just 20 of the Administration’s deregulatory actions will save U.S. consumers and businesses over $220 billion per year.

🎬 [WATCH LIVE: President Trump speaks on deregulation at 4 p.m. ET](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=aec088c685&e=3ee4985eea)

**What does that mean** for your family? According to CEA, President Trump’s deregulation program is projected to boost household incomes by $3,100 annually in the coming years. The benefits will take many forms: Americans will have access to cheaper cars, and patients will save nearly 10 percent on prescription drugs.

**Most important,** these rollbacks on everyday items will help blue-collar and middle-class Americans significantly more than the richest citizens of our country.

**Overregulation** falls disproportionately on the shoulders of lower-income families, who spend a larger share of their incomes on heavily regulated goods and services. Those purchases include transportation, food, and healthcare. Such government burdens also cost American jobs by causing workers to be replaced with lower-cost machines.

🎬 [Larry Kudlow: President Trump is getting blue-collar Americans back to work! ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=25f6ba24f8&e=3ee4985eea)

**Before taking office,** President Trump promised to roll back two regulations for every new one added in Washington. He’s kept that promise—and more. Under the Trump Administration, seven regulations have been rolled back for each new one implemented.

**Under****President Trump,** $50 billion in regulatory costs has already been saved. By current projections, the CEA estimates that cutting red tape will lead real incomes for Americans to rise by $53 billion per year between 2021 and 2029.

**Americans** **don’t need** Washington to create more white-collar jobs for central planners. They need a government that operates efficiently, effectively, and inexpensively to protect citizens while creating jobs for workers across the country.

🎬 [President Trump is giving every citizen a shot at the American Dream](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5c7dfef449&e=3ee4985eea)

**MORE:**[President Trump cuts environmental review time from 4.5 to 2 years](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0513c01715&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day
---
|
---
| **President Trump disembarks Air Force One at Joint Base Andrews** | July 15, 2020
---
|

name: Team Biden
subject: cutting it close
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
We've done the math. |
---
Peter, we'll keep this short:

You've heard a lot about our mid-month fundraising deadline, and how critical it is that we hit our 150,000 online donation goal before midnight.

We were on track to reach that goal, but based on today's donation rate, we're going to be cutting it _really close_.

**We've done the math, and if just 900 people from Pennsylvania make a donation today, we'll catch back up, hit our goal, and ensure that this campaign has the resources it needs to compete with Donald Trump's massive fundraising operation.**

[**So Peter, can you be one of the 900 people from Pennsylvania who chip in before midnight? body:

Peter,

My grandmother was the first woman to serve on the Dallas City Council, and I look forward to following in her footsteps and serving you in Congress.

I never thought I'd run for public office. I never wanted to see my face on TV. But I love this country, and like I did after we were attacked on 9/11, I'm stepping forward to serve in a crisis.
While our opponents are slinging mud in their expensive and white-hot-race-to-the-bottom primary, my message is simple. I'm running for Senate because Washington is failing Tennessee and we can't afford to elect another rubber stamp for the folks that are hurting our state.
Since I'm not taking any corporate PAC money, I need your help to keep this ad on the air. This campaign continues to run on grassroots support from folks like you, and it always will. I'm running to serve people, not special interests, and that's the way it should be.
[_**Please chip in today to help me on my mission to serve "All of America," and keep our ad on the air.**_](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa0ce/18d6a294/4c81630a/26d7f51f/3917466587/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIxMDc0MjMiLCJlM3R6YkhWbmZYMD0iOiJqbWUyMDA3MTZubyIsImUzdDJaWEp6YVc5dWZYMD0iOiJhZF9ubyJ9/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jme200716no?refcode=jme200716no_ad_no_other&refcode2=107423")

James

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Our jobs
body:
Your RI COVID-19 News Update
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
Yesterday, I shared with you [my vision for rebuilding Rhode Island's economy](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001OJXmvcIi39oK1Wu9EHzeceADujlMO5YpkB1OVu5y0r0TApQ6A9qbkjMqEbXO9XPwrKpf0nqcBjkOyqJSDtjxnsnZj23MFQwV-jEsNfQkyBKuH1kcLBXPKmdrUlXgsmnFG_WQLkmcHgY-e1S6pTRJCz3LqmvaG7j_g13mov80eE4Edt0Z0oCaCRJzQfGnBzOs1sdLRhcuQWEQxDvb5r-_H_6Y4gzhciy80xetBGCOv5eRY4BAIHzNtw==&c=1qAiEpICgdaI6NXW3RB9BBgIueS0Zs85Pj3JgLmEbtfv2dzoYVmP5g==&ch=n33LjIYL2YA1nobRKiYqld2KgXNqALmnS0AcSLEvClVgJBkwHiuOcw==) after the COVID-19 pandemic. We need to embrace what we've learned, and not just go back to the old way of doing business. The fundamental makeup of our economy has changed. We can't shy away from the hard truth that a lot of the jobs we've lost aren't coming back.
But there's another truth that we can't turn a blind eye to either: the old way of doing business left a lot of people behind. It left behind people without a degree or credential past high school who couldn't find a career; people who worked full-time and still lived in poverty; people of color who were held back by systemic racism. A plan that only gets us back to where we were still fails all of those people.
These fault lines existed for generations, but the pandemic has split them wide open. In the last six years, we've made progress creating jobs, revitalizing manufacturing, and making our economy more fair. Now, as we look to Rhode Island's future, we must commit to a vision of shared prosperity. We have an opportunity to position Rhode Island for success in growth industries like IT, design, and renewable energy, and to train Rhode Islanders for those jobs. Our new economy will build on the work of the past six years and put workers first - providing training and wraparound supports that give every Rhode Islander the opportunity to succeed.
Let's address these inequities head-on, together. Let's build a better, more equitable, more innovative economy. That requires making major investments in education, job training, small businesses, and housing. I'm excited to announce that I'll be dedicating more than $200 million in federal stimulus funding to support these programs.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001OJXmvcIi39oK1Wu9EHzeceADujlMO5YpkB1OVu5y0r0TApQ6A9qbkkX01Pa5czte1szIswa9JL_mxA171AcV61O5Ewxdz46r3zlGKgTO9MBG7kX9cpvFhumA9LpVkZMEiNchl3Nqy0Rdxn8G7hioDCOSR7miLAe-0v049EtKxNszeIZXQA_lY8Lwb1aFCkIZK8an1G1fnO4=&c=1qAiEpICgdaI6NXW3RB9BBgIueS0Zs85Pj3JgLmEbtfv2dzoYVmP5g==&ch=n33LjIYL2YA1nobRKiYqld2KgXNqALmnS0AcSLEvClVgJBkwHiuOcw==)
---
| Click the graph to see check the Department of Health's [COVID-19 data dashboard](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001OJXmvcIi39oK1Wu9EHzeceADujlMO5YpkB1OVu5y0r0TApQ6A9qbkkX01Pa5czte1szIswa9JL_mxA171AcV61O5Ewxdz46r3zlGKgTO9MBG7kX9cpvFhumA9LpVkZMEiNchl3Nqy0Rdxn8G7hioDCOSR7miLAe-0v049EtKxNszeIZXQA_lY8Lwb1aFCkIZK8an1G1fnO4=&c=1qAiEpICgdaI6NXW3RB9BBgIueS0Zs85Pj3JgLmEbtfv2dzoYVmP5g==&ch=n33LjIYL2YA1nobRKiYqld2KgXNqALmnS0AcSLEvClVgJBkwHiuOcw==).

| You can read more about my plan in the email I sent to you yesterday. clean_body: Your RI COVID-19 News Update | | | | | | | Hello friend, Yesterday, I shared with you == after the COVID-19 pandemic. We need to embrace what we've learned, and not just go back to the old way of doing business. The fundamental makeup of our economy has changed. We can't shy away from the hard truth that a lot of the jobs we've lost aren't coming back. But there's another truth that we can't turn a blind eye to either: the old way of doing business left a lot of people behind. It left behind people without a degree or credential past high school who couldn't find a career; people who worked full-time and still lived in poverty; people of color who were held back by systemic racism. A plan that only gets us back to where we were still fails all of those people. These fault lines existed for generations, but the pandemic has split them wide open. In the last six years, we've made progress creating jobs, revitalizing manufacturing, and making our economy more fair. Now, as we look to Rhode Island's future, we must commit to a vision of shared prosperity. We have an opportunity to position Rhode Island for success in growth industries like IT, design, and renewable energy, and to train Rhode Islanders for those jobs. Our new economy will build on the work of the past six years and put workers first - providing training and wraparound supports that give every Rhode Islander the opportunity to succeed. Let's address these inequities head-on, together. Let's build a better, more equitable, more innovative economy. That requires making major investments in education, job training, small businesses, and housing. I'm excited to announce that I'll be dedicating more than $200 million in federal stimulus funding to support these programs. | == | Click the graph to see check the Department of Health's ==. | You can read more about my plan in the email I sent to you yesterday. If you didn't catch it, you can read it here: ==. I'd love to hear your thoughts on it - changing the future of our state will take all of us working together. -Gina Raimondo Governor P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19, If you or someone you know wants to sign up to receive these emails, you can use ==. | | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903 | | Sent by mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov in collaboration with |

name: =?utf-8?Q?Jake=20LaTurner?=
subject: Congressman Ron Estes Endorsement
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kansans for LaTurner
body:
| |
---
| | | Friend,

It has been an incredible day. I am humbled to announce that this afternoon Congressman Ron Estes came to Topeka to announce that he is endorsing me in the race for the 2nd congressional district seat!

I am truly grateful to Ron for his example of leadership with integrity. In his short time in Congress, he’s earned the recognition of being a distinguished and proven leader, having been selected to serve on the influential Ways and Means Committee and gaining the respect of his colleagues and fellow Kansans.
---
|
---
| Here is some of what Congressman Estes had to say at our live news conference this afternoon:
---
| | **_"Jake LaTurner is a trusted conservative who is ready to represent eastern Kansas in Congress. There’s no question that he will serve Kansans in the 2nd District well – focusing on the issues that matter most like limiting the federal government, securing our country, balancing the budget, and protecting the unborn. Kansans deserve the best representation in Congress and Steve Watkins’ actions have unfortunately put this seat in danger of being handed over to a Nancy Pelosi liberal who doesn’t represent our Kansas values."_** **_\- Congressman Ron Estes_**
---
| There are less than 20 days until the primary election where voters will decide which Republican they want challenging a very formidable, well-funded Democrat. We must elect a Republican who can win, and I am the candidate who can do it.

[You can help support our campaign for Congress by donating at this link.](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=41cf79c6f6&e=1050d051da)

Yours in the fight,

Jake LaTurner

body:
We can't post on one of our biggest fundraising platforms.

|

| | Peter, a number of Twitter accounts were hacked yesterday, and as a precaution Twitter locked a number more. Here's the thing: Twitter is a valuable fundraising platform, and yesterday was our BIGGEST fundraising day online since launching this campaign. We can't afford to lose momentum.

Sen. Cornyn had access restored to his account and has been tweeting all day while ours has been locked, so we're counting on you to make up the gap.

**[Can you pitch in and make sure that we keep up the momentum?](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/64890110/7f51f7bc/41f288ca/4ef650fc/2913652214/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE3NzEifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-postrunoffb?refcode=email_20200716.3nd__1771__&amount=10")** \-- MJ

However, we know this is not an easy time for many. We'll still keep in touch with important information and to ask your thoughts from time to time.

_NOW:_ Amy McGrath has a ONE-POINT lead over Mitch McConnell and outraised him this past quarter -- As a result, her fundraising is DEAD EVEN with McConnell for the first time! Even James Carville admitted: "**I have NEVER seen a better opportunity to get rid of Mitch McConnell.** "

_BUT!:_ McConnell is executing an **$11 MILLION attack ad buy** to DESTROY Amy McGrath's momentum and secure his political survival now that they're neck-and-neck.
